rulings.Case Title: VY NGUYEN ET AL VS. JANET KRAHLING ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Good Faith Settlement Determination
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 6, 2024 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT THE MILLENNIUM TOWER ASSOCIATION, NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT DETERMINATION is OFF CALENDAR. The motion purports to be based inter alia on "the attached Declaration of Charles Hansen." (See Notice of Motion 3:18). There is document on file titled "Declaration of Charles H. Hansen" filed concurrently with the Notice of Motion on December 28, 2023. The body of this purported declaration consists of 10 lines of text without any required declaration language and starts with the following sentence: "To all parties herein and to their attorneys of record: Please take notice that on January 26, 2024, or as soon thereafter as this matter be hearing in department 302 of the above-entitled court. " =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ESSEX PORTFOLIO LP VS. MIGUEL ANGEL PICO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 6, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT TIFFANY GRADIN MOTION TO VACATE is OFF CALENDAR, no proof of service on file; motion is not set for hearing in accordance with CCP 1005; motion is moot. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
